author: @thagrit
text: Robert, by any chance have you gone any farther with the monotherm paints? I see you are about to make the Forward Working Group version of the power wall. As soon as you get into production on the caps . I am thinking the monotherm and power bank combo is on my top 1 list. Just out of curiosity have you tried cooling that monotherm to see if it stops making power? Might it flip polarity or just stop like chemo electric sources tend to do when chilled?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +thagrit Yes mate - I am running one now - a small one - I have some materials on order that are due in today or tomorrow and i will do a larger version and a video. I am organising a beta test with Carol Lovell and a friend in Oz

replies.author: @thagrit
replies.text: +Robert Murray-Smith Sounds awesome to me. thanks

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +thagrit cheers mate

author: @geodeaholicm4889
text: i fully understand & applaud your desire to repurpose & reuse your old desks.  just throwing them out to the landfill would be very wastefull.  the table you built is stylish & useful & i bet very satisfying.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +GEODEAHOLIC M it's getting a lot of use mate and we love it - but then anything you put effort into you tend to treat with more respect
